title: swertia racemosa
intro: Swertia racemosa, also known as Gilo or Kutki, is a perennial plant native to the Himalayas and the Indian subcontinent, belonging to the Gentianaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, including its bitter and anthelmintic properties, which aid in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Swertia racemosa include its ability to treat digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve respiratory health. The herbal preparations of Swertia racemosa include the root, which is used to make decoctions, powders, and capsules, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
